title: quercus robur var. tubulosa
intro: Quercus robur var. tubulosa, also known as the English oak, is a variety of the pedunculate oak tree native to Europe. The leaves, bark, and roots of Quercus robur var. tubulosa have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus robur var. tubulosa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies such as ointments and salves for topical application.
